title: Conjecture 4.2
statement_text: For any $1\leq s\leq\frac{\sqrt{r}}{\log r}$ and $n$ large enough we have $f(n,r,s)=\frac{s^{2}(\log r)^{1-o_{r}(1)}}{r}\cdot n$.
context_text: The paper determines $f(n,r,s)$ — the maximum number of vertices guaranteably connected using up to $s$ colours in any $r$-edge colouring of $K_n$ — up to a logarithmic factor. The upper and lower bounds in the regime $s\leq\sqrt{r}/\log r$ differ by an absolute constant between 4 and 8 depending on divisibility properties of $r$; this conjecture posits the precise asymptotic behaviour in that regime. The authors note the gap originates in the proof technique and that under appropriate divisibility assumptions the result can be sharpened to within a factor of $2+\varepsilon$.

title: Liu–Morris–Prince Conjecture
statement_text: [Verbatim statement not reproduced in the provided input; see H. Liu, R. Morris, and N. Prince, Discrete Math.\ 308 (2008), no.\ 22, 5096–5121.]
context_text: Liu, Morris and Prince [36] established benchmarks for the behaviour of $f(n,r,s)$ (highly connected multicoloured subgraphs of complete graphs) and posed both an open problem and a conjecture. The present paper resolves the open problem and disproves their conjecture in a strong form.
paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture of Liu, Morris, and Prince in a strong form, and additionally solves an open problem posed in the same paper.
notes: Statement not reproduced in the provided input excerpt. Attribution and year inferred from bibliography entry [36]. The abstract confirms the conjecture is disproved, so it is no longer open.
